project.name: Milking Process Compliance Monitor
project.revision: v2 - addresses gaps identified in v1 review
planning_assumptions: The MVP monitors only the two cow positions visible to the fixed side camera.
The system verifies task occurrence, sequence, and duration, not execution quality.
Employee identity is supervisor-assigned in MVP and may be edited post-hoc, with the change logged.
Real-time inference runs in a separate Python service and feeds events to the web app via a backend-owned ingestion layer.
Low-confidence detections are classified as unverifiable, not missed, to avoid penalizing employees for camera blind spots.

known_gaps.reason: Sizing requires real team velocity data, which this planning pass does not have. Treat this as a dependency graph and scope document, not a schedule, until estimation is done separately.

data_model_additions.note: Additions to the entities defined in the source spec, made to address v1 review findings.

entities.name: TaskEvent
fields_added: status now includes 'unverifiable' in addition to 'completed' and 'missed'
override_fields: override_reason (nullable, required if override_status is set)
entities.note: Original detected status/confidence is never overwritten by an override. The override is stored alongside the original for audit purposes; the UI displays the effective status (override if present, else detected) but both are queryable.

entities.name: AuditLog
entities.note: Written on employee reassignment (Session.employee_id changes) and on any TaskEvent override.

phases.name: Discovery and scope lock
phases.goal: Convert the domain requirements into implementation-ready rules, data contracts, and success criteria before building core features.
objectives: Confirm pilot scope and sign off on the two-cow camera limitation.
Define task timing thresholds, anomaly thresholds, and missed-task rules with a milking supervisor.
Define confidence-score thresholds that distinguish 'missed' (high-confidence no-action) from 'unverifiable' (low-confidence/occluded) task detections.
Freeze the task taxonomy, session boundaries, and event semantics used by the backend and inference service.
Define the labeling format needed for pilot data collection and model tuning.
Confirm on-site infrastructure readiness: camera installation, RTSP endpoint reachability, and network stability.
deliverables: Signed scope document for pilot constraints and exclusions.
Task event contract describing start, end, missed, unverifiable, and anomaly states, including confidence-score thresholds.
Labeling guide for segment-level task annotations.
Initial acceptance criteria for the MVP pilot.
Site infrastructure readiness checklist (camera, network, RTSP endpoint).
risks: Thresholds may be too loose or too strict without supervisor input.
Labeling effort may be underestimated because segment labeling is more expensive than box labeling.
Site infrastructure may not be pilot-ready, blocking phase_3 development.
acceptance_criteria: Client confirms the 2-cow pilot scope and agrees that quality verification is out of scope.
Each of the six tasks has a clear detection rule, miss rule, unverifiable rule, and anomaly rule.

A minimal dataset plan exists for both monitored positions under different barn conditions, specifying a minimum labeled-footage benchmark (hours, positions covered, minimum number of distinct employees) required before MVP thresholds are finalized.
Camera and network infrastructure on site is confirmed functional and reachable for development and testing.

phases.name: Platform foundation
phases.goal: Establish the application, database, authentication, validation, security baseline, and event transport layer needed by all later work.
objectives: Create the Next.js App Router foundation with the UI stack specified in the spec.
Set up Clerk authentication for supervisor sign-in and sign-up pages.
Implement the PostgreSQL and Drizzle schema for Supervisor, Employee, Session, CowProcess, TaskEvent (with confidence_score and override fields), AuditLog, and Report.
Add Zod validation, sanitization, CSRF protection, and API rate limiting.
Build the event ingestion API that will receive events from the inference service, persist them, and broadcast them to connected dashboard clients.
Create the shared application layout, navigation, and empty states for the main sections.
deliverables: Working authenticated shell for supervisors.
Database migrations and typed models for the core entities, including AuditLog.
Reusable server-side validation and authorization helpers.
Event ingestion API + persistence + WebSocket/SSE broadcast layer (owned here; consumed by phase_3's publisher and phase_4's dashboard).
Base UI layout for scheduling, live monitoring, statistics, and reports.
risks: Auth and database modeling can drift if the session lifecycle is not locked early.
Security middleware may need refactoring if API shapes change later.
If the event transport layer's contract isn't stable before phase_3 starts, both workstreams may build against mismatched assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: A supervisor can authenticate and reach a protected application shell.
Core entities are persisted with server-side validation and authorization checks.
State-changing routes are protected by CSRF and rate limiting.
The event ingestion API can accept a test event and broadcast it to a connected client end-to-end, independent of the real inference service being ready.

phases.name: Scheduling and session management
phases.goal: Deliver the supervisor workflow for creating and managing a monitoring session before milking begins.
objectives: Build the four-step scheduling form with client and server Zod validation.
Persist session drafts and finalized sessions with editable assigned employee data.
Log every employee reassignment to AuditLog (old value, new value, who, when).
Support session status transitions for scheduled, active, completed, and ended_early.
Add supervisor identity capture for session attribution.
Create session management views for editing, viewing, and re-opening session records, including a visible edit history.
deliverables: Multi-step session creation flow.
Session detail page with editable metadata.
Stored supervisor and employee assignment workflow with audit logging.
Status transition logic for session lifecycle management.
Edit-history view on the session detail page, sourced from AuditLog.
risks: Schedule edits near session start may complicate downstream inference triggers.
acceptance_criteria: A supervisor can create a session with start time, estimated end time, cow counts, employee, and identity details.
Session edits are persisted safely, logged to AuditLog, and remain visible in the dashboard, statistics, and edit-history views.
The app can represent an active live watch window separate from the task-level detection state.

phases.name: Inference service MVP
phases.goal: Implement the separate Python service that ingests RTSP video, detects objects, and emits task events for the two monitored cow positions.
objectives: Set up FastAPI, OpenCV, and Ultralytics YOLOv8 ingestion for a live RTSP stream.
Split each frame into two regions of interest matching the monitored cow positions.
Implement the rule-based temporal state machine for the six milking tasks.
Detect cow-process start and end boundaries within the supervisor-defined session window.
Emit structured events for task started, task completed, missed task, unverifiable task, anomaly flagged, cow process started, and cow process completed, each carrying a confidence_score.
Classify each task event as completed, missed, or unverifiable using the confidence thresholds defined in phase_0.
Publish events to the ingestion API built in phase_1, tagged correctly with session_id and cow_position.
deliverables: Standalone inference service with configuration for stream source and thresholds.
Task-event schema shared with the backend, including confidence_score and the unverifiable status.
State machine implementation for the six tasks across two cow positions.
Event publisher integrated with the phase_1 ingestion API.
Integration test suite verifying events are correctly tagged to session_id/cow_position and correctly gated by the session's watch window.
risks: Occlusion may cause false negatives for cleaning, stripping, and post-dip — mitigated by the unverifiable classification, but the confidence threshold itself needs real tuning data.
The first model pass may detect object presence but not enough temporal precision without tuning.
Stream stability and frame timing may affect task boundary inference.
Insufficient labeled footage at this stage could produce unreliable thresholds; do not finalize thresholds until the phase_0 labeled-footage benchmark is met.
acceptance_criteria: The service can watch a live stream and produce deterministic per-ROI task events.
Attachment and detachment events are detected with high confidence.
The service distinguishes between task occurrence and task quality limitations exactly as the spec requires.
Low-confidence detections due to occlusion are classified as unverifiable, not missed.
The integration test suite passes: events reliably arrive at the backend correctly tagged and time-gated before phase_4 dashboard work begins.

phases.name: Real-time dashboard and analytics
phases.goal: Expose live session state to supervisors, allow correction of detection errors, and provide historical analytics for operational review.
objectives: Build a real-time dashboard powered by WebSocket or SSE updates from the phase_1 ingestion/broadcast layer.
Show current session progress, current task per cow position, and per-task completion state, distinguishing completed / missed / unverifiable visually.
Allow supervisors to correct the assigned employee on the live session when needed (writes to AuditLog).
Allow supervisors to override a task's status (e.g. unverifiable or missed -> completed) with a required reason note, without deleting the original detected status.
Create the statistics page with filtering by date range, employee, and cow position.
Surface missed-task alerts, unverifiable-task notices, and duration anomalies as they occur.
deliverables: Live monitoring UI for the active session.
Manual override UI for task status corrections, backed by AuditLog.
Historical statistics dashboard.
Filterable summary views for task counts, durations, and employee comparison.
Live update transport wired to the phase_1 broadcast layer, with reconnect/retry handling.
risks: Realtime transport may need retry and reconnect handling to avoid stale views.
Analytics accuracy depends on consistent event emission from the inference service.
If phase_3's integration test suite has not passed, dashboard development risks building against unreliable event data.
acceptance_criteria: A supervisor can watch live cow-process state update within a few seconds of event emission.
Missed tasks, unverifiable tasks, and anomalies are visible immediately and distinguishably on the dashboard.
A supervisor can override a task's status with a required reason, and the override is visible alongside the original detected status in the audit history.
Historical filtering works for sessions, employees, tasks, and monitored positions.

phases.name: Reporting and notifications
phases.goal: Automate report generation and end-of-session communication for supervisors.
objectives: Generate Word reports for a selected session or date range using the recorded task data.
Send transactional email to the supervisor when a session ends.
Include session summary, task durations, missed/unverifiable/overridden tasks, and per-cow-process completion details in the report output.
Persist report metadata and generated file location in the database.
deliverables: Docx report generator, clearly distinguishing detected-missed, unverifiable, and supervisor-overridden task outcomes.
Email notification flow using Resend or Nodemailer.
Report record linked to the session.
Download action from the statistics or session detail view.
risks: Report content may need formatting refinement for client-facing readability.
Email delivery can fail independently of session completion and should not block the workflow.
Conflating unverifiable and missed in the report would misrepresent employee performance — must remain visually/textually distinct.
acceptance_criteria: A completed session produces a downloadable .docx report.
The supervisor receives a session-end email with the main summary data.
Report generation works for both single-session and filtered-range views.
The report clearly separates missed, unverifiable, and overridden task outcomes rather than merging them into one 'non-completed' bucket.

phases.name: Pilot hardening and rollout
phases.goal: Stabilize the system for the first on-farm pilot and prepare a controlled launch.
objectives: Test the end-to-end flow under varied lighting, weather, and barn conditions.
Tune thresholds (including missed/unverifiable confidence cutoffs) using pilot recordings and supervisor review.
Add logging, error handling, and operator-friendly fallback states.
Document deployment, restart, and troubleshooting steps for the operator.
Run a narrow pilot with the two monitored cow positions before any scaling effort.
deliverables: Validated pilot deployment checklist.
Threshold tuning notes and known false-positive/false-negative/unverifiable-rate cases.
Operational runbook for supervisors and maintainers.
Pilot feedback log for future iteration, including how often supervisors used the manual override.
risks: The pilot may expose more false negatives/unverifiable rates than expected because the camera angle is fixed and occluded.
If event timing is noisy, the report layer may need stricter normalization rules.
A high override rate may indicate thresholds need retuning rather than treating overrides as one-off corrections.
acceptance_criteria: The system can run a full pilot session end-to-end without manual intervention beyond supervisor session setup and legitimate corrections.
Known limitations are documented clearly for the client and operators.
Operational issues and override-rate patterns are captured in a backlog for the next iteration.

phases.name: Phase 2 expansion roadmap
phases.goal: Prepare the next funded iteration that addresses the explicit limitations left out of the MVP.
objectives: Add additional cameras or angles for full-row coverage and reduced occlusion.
Evaluate a learned temporal model to replace or augment the rule-based state machine, informed by pilot override-rate and unverifiable-rate data.
Introduce optional employee identity cross-checking such as face recognition if legally and operationally approved.
Consider multi-role access and employee-facing views if the business asks for them.
Add non-essential visual polish such as Three.js only after core reliability is stable.
deliverables: Expansion requirements brief.
Camera coverage and infrastructure proposal.
ML evolution plan based on pilot label quality, override rates, and collected sessions.
Feature prioritization for the next funding phase.
phases.note: Can be scoped in parallel with any phase from phase_1 onward — it does not need to wait for phase_6 to begin planning. Implementation of expansion items, however, should wait until pilot learnings from phase_6 exist.
risks: Scaling before the pilot proves the thresholds may produce unreliable analytics.
Identity verification introduces regulatory and consent complexity.
acceptance_criteria: The team has a funded, ordered backlog for the next phase of product growth.
Every MVP limitation has a corresponding expansion path or explicit reason for deferral.

cross_cutting_workstreams.name: Data and labeling
items: Collect representative sessions across multiple employees and barn conditions.
Define segment labels for task starts and ends per cow process.
Track labeling quality and disagreements to improve threshold tuning.
Do not finalize missed/unverifiable confidence thresholds until the phase_0 labeled-footage benchmark is met.

cross_cutting_workstreams.name: Security and compliance
items: Enforce server-side authorization for every session, report, and edit operation.
Validate and sanitize all form and API inputs.
Apply rate limiting to auth and submission endpoints.
Document any future consent requirements before identity-related enhancements.

cross_cutting_workstreams.name: Observability
items: Log inference service state transitions and event emissions.
Trace missing or duplicated task events back to their source frame windows.
Monitor transport failures between the inference service and backend.
Track override frequency per task type as a signal for threshold retuning.

cross_cutting_workstreams.name: Fairness and auditability
items: Never conflate 'unverifiable due to occlusion' with 'missed due to employee action' in any UI, report, or stats aggregation.
Every change to employee assignment or task status is logged to AuditLog with who/when/why.
Original ML-detected status is never overwritten by a supervisor override — both remain queryable.

mvp_in_scope: Supervisor authentication and protected app shell
Drizzle/PostgreSQL persistence for the core entities, including AuditLog
Event ingestion, persistence, and broadcast layer
Python inference service with YOLOv8 and rule-based state machine, including confidence-scored missed/unverifiable classification
Live dashboard for the two monitored cow positions, with manual override capability
Statistics page and Word export, distinguishing missed/unverifiable/overridden outcomes
mvp_out_of_scope: Full-row coverage beyond the two monitored cow positions
Quality verification of cleaning, stripping, and post-dip coverage
Learned temporal modeling as the primary detection strategy
Decorative 3D visuals before core reliability is stable
Formal effort/timeline estimation (flagged as a separate required exercise)
